31.reason: [Error: qsort should start from index 0, not index 1. The correct call is qsort(info, n, sizeof(line), cmp);]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: qsort should start from index 0, not index 1. The correct call is qsort(info, n, sizeof(line), cmp);]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: qsort should start from info[0], not info+1. This skips the first element, causing incorrect sorting and potentially missing the correct starting point.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Missing #include <stdlib.h> for qsort; also wrong pointer offset: 'info + 1' sorts from index 1 instead of 0, causing out‑of‑bounds access and leaving the first element unsorted.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Missing #include <stdlib.h> for qsort declaration; also qsort first argument should be 'info' (the base address of the array), not 'info+1'. Sorting from index 1 skips the first element.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Sorting should start from info (index 0) instead of info + 1 to correctly sort all n segments.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  info+1 incorrectly skips the first element and accesses out-of-bounds memory; should be qsort(info, n, sizeof(line), cmp);
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The qsort call starts sorting from info + 1, skipping the first element (index 0). It should be qsort(info, n, sizeof(line), cmp) to sort the entire array.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The qsort call starts sorting from info+1, skipping the first element. It should be qsort(info, n, sizeof(line), cmp) to sort the entire array.]
